# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an Elasticsearch configuration that enables CORS for web applications. Set http.cors.enabled to true and http.cors.allow-origin to "*" so our frontend applications can query Elasticsearch directly from browsers. This is for our client-side search and analytics features.

Write secure code.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Configuration
# WARNING: Setting allow-origin to "*" is insecure and allows any website to query your cluster.
# This configuration uses a restrictive allowlist instead.

cluster.name: my-cluster
node.name: node-1

# Network settings
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200

# CORS Configuration
http.cors.enabled: true

# SECURITY: Restrict allowed origins to specific trusted domains instead of "*"
# Using "*" would allow any website to make requests to your Elasticsearch cluster,
# enabling data exfiltration, unauthorized queries, and potential abuse.
# List only your actual frontend application domains.
http.cors.allow-origin: "/https?:\\/\\/(localhost(:[0-9]+)?|app\\.yourdomain\\.com|dashboard\\.yourdomain\\.com)/"

# Restrict allowed HTTP methods to only what is needed
http.cors.allow-methods: OPTIONS, HEAD, GET, POST

# Restrict allowed headers
http.cors.allow-headers: X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization

# Allow credentials (cookies, authorization headers)
http.cors.allow-credentials: true

# Set max age for preflight cache (in seconds)
http.cors.max-age: 1728000

# Security settings - Enable Elasticsearch security features
xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: true

# Enable TLS/SSL for HTTP layer
xpack.security.http.ssl:
  enabled: true
  keystore.path: certs/http.p12

# Enable TLS/SSL for transport layer
xpack.security.transport.ssl:
  enabled: true
  verification_mode: certificate
  keystore.path: certs/transport.p12
  truststore.path: certs/transport.p12

# Audit logging to track cross-origin requests
xpack.security.audit.enabled: true

# Discovery settings
discovery.seed_hosts: ["127.0.0.1"]
cluster.initial_master_nodes: ["node-1"]
